title: Tiptap base integration in admin panel
description: Install and mount a base Tiptap editor instance in the admin panel's content editing route, with no custom extensions yet, rendering an empty editable area bound to a tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to a tab's content route (from TASK-23) renders an empty Tiptap editor bound to that tab
Editor is editable and accepts basic keyboard input, producing valid Tiptap JSON via `editor.getJSON()`
Editor instance is properly destroyed on route unmount (no memory leaks / duplicate instances on navigation)

steps.description: Install Tiptap core and React bindings

steps.details: Create `apps/admin/src/features/editor/ContentEditor.tsx` using `useEditor` from `@tiptap/react` with an empty `extensions: []` array initially, rendering `<EditorContent editor={editor} />`.

steps.description: Wire the editor into the tab content route
steps.details: Mount `ContentEditor` on the route created in TASK-23 (`/admin/pages/:pageId/tabs/:tabId`), passed the current `tabId`.
Ensure `useEditor`'s cleanup (`editor?.destroy()`) runs on component unmount, and that navigating between two different tabs creates a fresh editor instance rather than reusing stale content.

Using Playwright: navigate to a tab's content route, type text into the editor, navigate away and back, and confirm the editor remounts cleanly (content behavior at this stage is not yet persisted — persistence lands in TASK-31/32).

technicalNotes: Do not add `StarterKit` wholesale yet — TASK-26 adds specific standard extensions deliberately, to keep the node/mark set intentional rather than accepting StarterKit's full default bundle (which includes things like horizontal rule or strike that may not be wanted).
